mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% as of May 2016 indicates near-zero rates
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor market conditions and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings. Positive month-over-month PAYEMS growth triggers positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to labor strength.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,539 thousand as of May 2016
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth months produce negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show concurrent month-over-month growth, the combined signal of robust household spending and construction activity produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand breadth that single-series signals miss.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show month-over-month growth simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 378,706 million and HOUST at 519 thousand as of April-May 2016
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Concurrent positive growth in both RSAFS and HOUST produces negative 20-day forward returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic output and capacity utilization. A sustained 3-month decline in INDPRO signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession warning
failure_condition: Three-month INDPRO declines produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. When UNRATE rises above 5.0%, it triggers a regime shift toward defensive positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure in anticipation of earnings pressure.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of May 2016, below 5.0% threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Months with UNRATE > 5.0% produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and monetary policy stance (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real interest rates. When CPIAUCSL growth accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates become negative, compressing equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors seek inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 219.146 and FEDFUNDS at 0.19% as of May 2016
regime_hint: low-rate environment with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day forward returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of aggregate demand. Quarterly GDPC1 growth above 2.0% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This is an always-on trend signal reflecting fundamental growth momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,277.4 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2016
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.0% produce negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead consumer spending and construction employment by 2-3 months. A sharp month-over-month decline in HOUST below 500 thousand units signals weakening housing demand and forward-looking consumer weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate demand contraction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500 thousand units or declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 519 thousand as of April 2016, near threshold
regime_hint: housing market slowdown or demand-side recession warning
failure_condition: HOUST below 500k or > 5% monthly declines produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
